Empires & Puzzles Matilda Review: The Unhallowed Pastry Chef Guide

Matilda may look sweet with her tray of muffins, but she serves a deadly course of poison and ailments. We analyze if her unique DoT amplification mechanics can still compete against the massive stats of modern heroes.
Matilda Review: The Queen of Poisonous Pastries & DoT
Matilda, The Unhallowed Pastry Chef: DoT Amplifier and Fiend Master

Matilda is a 5-star Legendary Holy (Yellow) hero from the Morlovia Family, originally released during the Halloween event of 2022. She plays a unique role as a "Damage over Time" (DoT) amplifier, designed to synergize with other ailment-heavy heroes rather than just hitting hard upfront. While her playful, spooky-cute aesthetic might suggest a support role, her "Muffin Poisoning" special skill can turn the tide of battle by making enemy ailments tick harder and last longer. However, as an older hero, she faces the harsh reality of power creep, fighting to stay relevant with stats that are now considered quite modest.

Matilda's design is a delightful blend of whimsical and eerie. Dressed as a pastry chef with a witchy twist, she presents a tray of "treats" that are clearly tricks—eyeballs, ghosts, and monstrous muffins. The vibrant purple and yellow color palette fits the Halloween theme perfectly without being overly dark. It's a refreshing, character-rich design that stands out from the usual lineup of armored knights and dragons, leaning into the "Return to Morlovia" playful horror vibe.
When comparing Matilda's stats to the modern landscape, the age gap is painfully obvious.
Modern attackers regularly exceed 1300 base attack. Matilda's damage output from tiles and direct hits is negligible by comparison.

This is her biggest liability. Modern support heroes often boast 2200+ HP and 1300+ Defense. Even with Limit Break 2 (reaching ~1836 HP), Matilda is significantly squishier than a non-limit-broken modern hero. She requires careful protection and possibly defensive emblem nodes to survive long enough to cast her average-speed skill.

She deals direct Poison damage over 3 turns. While decent, it's not a game-ender on its own.

She can become functionally Fast (9 tiles) fairly easily, especially if paired with another Morlovia hero.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Morlovia family bonus provides a Mana Generation boost (+5% for 1 hero, +12% for 2, etc.). This is excellent. Pairing Matilda with another Morlovia hero (like Alucard or Miriam & Midnight) instantly makes her faster and more consistent.
When casting her special, she has a

to steal the newest dispellable buff from a random enemy. This is a fantastic utility passive. If she steals a Taunt, a Dodge, or a massive Defense Up, it can swing the momentum instantly. If no buffs are present, she deals extra damage, which is a nice consolation prize.
At the start of battle, she gets a defense boost. Given her outdated stats, this is arguably the best Aether Power she could ask for, helping her survive the initial barrage of fast snipers.

Upon release, Matilda was a "sleeper" hit. Players eventually discovered her synergy with "Mindless Attack" heroes (like Onatel or Alfrike) and the then-50% refresh chance created infinite lockdown loops. She became a niche powerhouse for Rush Attack wars and specific PvE content.
Her unique mechanic (DoT amplification) has aged well because it scales with
heroes' damage. However, her survivability has crashed. She dies to stray tiles or splash damage from modern heavy hitters.
She is no longer a general-purpose hero. You cannot drop her into a standard defense team. However, in
events where you need to melt massive boss HP bars with DoT, she remains a potent tool if protected.

Amplifying DoT damage is a valid strategy for high scores if you lack pure raw damage dealers.

The goal with Matilda is to pair her with heroes who apply strong, long-lasting ailments.

Matilda applies the +50% weakness. Rayne applies massive Poison (which stacks with Matilda's because they are different sources/IDs often, or refresh each other). The Fire DoT adds another layer. The enemy takes 1500+ damage per turn without you touching a tile.

Grimble, Captain of Diamonds, or any Minion/Fiend destroyer.
Heroes who destroy fiends cheaply remove her main utility immediately.
Don't pair her with heroes who overwrite strong DoTs with weak ones (though newer heroes usually have superior DoTs).

If you pulled Matilda early, she is a fantastic boss killer for Map Stages. Her fiends will keep bosses from healing, and her poison will whittle them down while you focus on survival. Level her up, but prioritize keeping her alive with healers.

She is a luxury toy. Do not prioritize Alpha Aethers for her unless you are a dedicated Rush War specialist or simply love playing DoT teams for fun. She cannot compete in the top 100 alliance wars due to her stats, but she remains a fun "win-more" card in lower-stakes raids.

The community was initially in love with her "infinite loop" potential (50% refresh). When SG nerfed it to 25%, the backlash was fierce. Many felt she was "neutered" unnecessarily compared to the raw power of heroes like Anne or Jove at the time.
"She looks like a duck, quacks like a duck, must be OP... until the nerf bat hit."
"Matilda is the most fun I’ve had in such a long time...
the nerf solidified my decision to never spend again."
- A common sentiment reflecting the frustration of investing in a fun mechanic only to see it changed.

Matilda remains one of the most mechanically interesting heroes in Empires & Puzzles. She shifts the gameplay from "match tiles, kill hero" to a more strategic "set traps, watch them wither" style. While her stats have gathered dust and her infinite loops are gone, the core joy of watching an enemy health bar melt from 100% to 0% purely from status ailments is a satisfaction only the Unhallowed Pastry Chef can truly deliver.
Keep for Rush and DoT fun. Bench for competitive defense.
A comprehensive review of Matilda, the 5-star Holy hero from the Morlovia Family. Analyze her DoT amplification, fiend mechanics, and viability in the modern meta.
